steps.title: Exactly one solution means discriminant $= 0$
steps.content: For $-9x^2-12x+c=0$, identify $a=-9$, $b=-12$, and constant term $c$. A quadratic has exactly one real solution only when the graph is tangent to the $x$-axis, which is the same as setting the discriminant equal to $0$.

steps.title: Discriminant shortcut: solve for $c$
steps.content:
$$(-12)^2-4(-9)(c)=0$$
$$144+36c=0$$
$$c=-4$$
The constant that makes the discriminant zero is -4.
